name: Washington Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank with 140+ years of history offering personal and business banking, loans, and wealth management services. FDIC-insured with locations in Illinois.
description_long:
Washington Savings Bank was founded in 1883 as The Washington Loan and Building Association to help members purchase homes. The organization evolved through multiple charter changes—becoming Washington Savings and Loans Association in 1956 and converting to Washington Savings Bank in 1992. The bank became federally insured in 1958 and has grown from initial assets of $4,703.65 to over $588 million as of December 2022. Recent growth includes acquisitions of the Effingham Keller Branch (2010), First Federal Savings and Loan of Mattoon (2015), and First National Bank Mattoon (2021).

Washington Savings Bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, mortgage loans, and personal loans. They provide business banking solutions and maintain a Wealth Management Department. The bank features Elite Rewards Checking with cash back on debit card purchases, virtual branch access, and scholarship administration through charitable trusts. Educational resources and security/identity theft information are available on their website.

The bank differentiates itself through its "Big Bank Solutions. Community Bank Values" positioning—combining modern banking tools with personalized community service. Their 140+ year history demonstrates institutional stability, and FDIC insurance provides deposit protection backed by the U.S. Government. The bank maintains multiple locations in Illinois (Mattoon and Effingham mentioned) and actively invests in community initiatives including scholarship programs.

As a regional community bank, Washington Savings Bank is best suited for customers seeking personal service and local presence rather than cutting-edge digital banking. The website content is limited in detail regarding specific loan products, interest rates, fees, or comprehensive service listings, making it difficult to assess competitive positioning on terms. Their primary strength lies in longevity, community roots, and FDIC protection rather than innovation or specialized lending products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Elite Rewards Checking with cash back on debit purchases
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinancing
Account login for business and personal customers
In-person service at Mattoon and Effingham locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
140+ years of operating history demonstrates stability and community roots since 1883
Elite Rewards Checking offers cash back on everyday debit card purchases with no category tracking required
Multiple locations in Illinois (Mattoon and Effingham) for in-person banking
Comprehensive services including personal banking, business banking, mortgages, and wealth management
Virtual branch available for remote account access and management
Community-focused with active scholarship program and charitable trusts
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Illinois locations only
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, fees, or APRs for products
No mention of online-only accounts or nationally competitive digital banking features
Limited details about loan products, terms, or lending criteria available on website
Smaller institution may have fewer branches and ATMs compared to national banks
best_for: Illinois residents seeking local community banking with personal service and established relationships
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and institutional stability over digital innovation
Homebuyers interested in mortgage lending from a bank with deep local market knowledge
Small business owners in central Illinois needing relationship-based business banking
diagnosis: Washington Savings Bank is best for Illinois residents and small business owners who value community-based banking with personal service, stability, and FDIC protection over national reach or digital-first banking. The primary caveat is limited geographic availability and a lack of transparent pricing information on the website, making it necessary to contact the bank directly for competitive rate and fee comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
